title: PolyEdge Production Transformation (sisyphus polyedge-production-transformation.md)
description: Convert sisyphus polyedge-production-transformation.md plan into structured stories. Existing RQ-001..RQ-018 cover the Phase-1 job queue layer. New PE-001..PE-014 cover the missing Phase 2-5 work: blockchain whale monitoring, news feeds, sentiment, dynamic whale discovery, auto-trader + risk manager, arbitrage detector, ML prediction scaffold, and the live trading terminal frontend.

stories.title: Create JobQueue database model
acceptanceCriteria: JobQueue table exists in backend/models/database.py with all fields: id, job_type, idempotency_key, priority, status, payload, retry_count, max_retries, scheduled_at, started_at, completed_at, error_message, created_at
Index idx_job_queue_status_priority exists on (status, priority)
UniqueConstraint uq_job_idempotency exists on (job_type, idempotency_key)
python -c "from backend.models.database import JobQueue; print(JobQueue.__tablename__)" outputs 'job_queue'
Table creation tested via Base.metadata.create_all()

stories.title: Create abstract queue and cache interfaces
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/abstract.py exists with AbstractQueue class
AbstractQueue defines enqueue(), dequeue(), complete(), fail(), get_pending_count()
backend/queue/abstract.py exists with AbstractCache class
AbstractCache defines get(), set(), delete(), exists(), flush()
Factory functions create_queue() and create_cache() implemented
Interfaces documented with clear type hints

stories.title: Implement AsyncSQLiteQueue with thread pool
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/sqlite_queue.py implements AsyncSQLiteQueue extending AbstractQueue
ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) created as _db_executor for sync DB operations
run_in_thread() wrapper executes sync DB calls in thread pool
enqueue() inserts job record with status='pending', scheduled_at=now
dequeue() fetches next pending job ordered by priority (CASE statement)
dequeue() updates job status='processing', started_at=now
complete() updates job status='completed', completed_at=now
fail() updates job status='failed', error_message set, retry_count incremented
get_pending_count() returns count of jobs with status='pending'
All methods are async and properly await thread pool operations

stories.title: Add job timeout mechanism
acceptanceCriteria: JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS config added to backend/config.py (default=300)
Worker loop wraps dispatch_job() in asyncio.wait_for(timeout=settings.JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
TimeoutError caught and logged when job exceeds timeout
Job marked as failed with timeout error message
Worker continues processing next job after timeout (no slot exhaustion)
Test verifies hung job times out and worker continues

stories.title: Implement job handlers for market scan and settlement
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/handlers.py created with market_scan() function
backend/queue/handlers.py created with settlement_check() function
backend/queue/handlers.py created with signal_generation() function
Each handler imports and uses existing scheduler functions (scan_and_trade_job, etc.)
Handlers properly handle exceptions and return results
Handlers are importable and don't block on startup

stories.title: Implement worker loop with graceful shutdown
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/worker.py created with Worker class
Worker._in_flight_jobs Set[int] tracks active job IDs
Worker.start() calls dequeue() in loop with max_concurrent=1 limit
Worker.start() dispatches jobs to handlers from backend/queue/handlers.py
Worker.stop() sets _running=False and waits for in-flight jobs (max 30s)
Worker.stop() calls _db_executor.shutdown(wait=True) for cleanup
MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS config respected (default=1)
Worker logs job lifecycle events (start, complete, fail, timeout)

stories.title: Integrate queue worker with scheduler
acceptanceCriteria: JOB_WORKER_ENABLED config added to backend/config.py (default=False)
backend/core/scheduler.py modified to check settings.JOB_WORKER_ENABLED
When JOB_WORKER_ENABLED=True: scheduler.remove_job() called for existing APScheduler jobs
start_scheduler() starts worker when JOB_WORKER_ENABLED=True
APScheduler kept only as timer (no direct job execution when worker enabled)
No double-execution of jobs (verified via logs)
Worker process starts successfully with scheduler

stories.title: Configure SQLite WAL mode for concurrent access
acceptanceCriteria: configure_sqlite_wal() function added to backend/models/database.py
SQLAlchemy event listener sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL on connect
PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 set for better concurrency
PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL set for queue workloads
WAL mode verified via sqlite3 job_queue.db "PRAGMA journal_mode" (outputs 'wal')
Multiple concurrent reads tested without blocking

stories.title: Create job queue migration script
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/migrate_jobs.py created with migrate_apjobs_to_queue() function
Script reads existing APScheduler jobs from scheduler state
Script enqueues each job to JobQueue table via AsyncSQLiteQueue
Script logs migration progress (jobs migrated, failures)
Migration script executable via python -m backend.queue.migrate_jobs
Existing APScheduler jobs successfully migrated to persistent queue

stories.title: Test crash recovery and job persistence
acceptanceCriteria: backend/tests/test_queue/test_crash_recovery.py created
Test enqueues 10 jobs, kills process after 3 complete
Test restarts and verifies remaining 7 jobs execute
Test verifies job_queue table shows all 10 jobs completed
Test passes with kill -9 signal simulation
Jobs survive process crash with data integrity intact

stories.title: Test job timeout and graceful shutdown
acceptanceCriteria: backend/tests/test_queue/test_worker.py created
Test creates job that sleeps 400s (>300s timeout)
Test verifies job times out and marked as failed
Test verifies worker continues processing after timeout
Test starts 5 long-running jobs and sends SIGTERM
Test verifies worker waits for jobs (max 30s) before shutdown
Test verifies _db_executor.shutdown(wait=True) called
All worker tests pass

stories.title: Test queue operations and idempotency
acceptanceCriteria: backend/tests/test_queue/test_sqlite_queue.py created
Test verifies enqueue() creates job record with correct fields
Test verifies dequeue() returns jobs in priority order (critical > high > medium > low)
Test verifies idempotency constraint prevents duplicate jobs
Test verifies complete() and fail() update job status correctly
Test verifies retry_count increments on failure
Test verifies get_pending_count() returns accurate count
All queue tests pass with >95% coverage

stories.title: Update Phase 1 plan with job queue work
acceptanceCriteria: phase-1-production-readiness.md updated to include job queue in Step 2
Revised Phase 1 effort: 5.5h → 8h (added SQLite queue + tests)
Job queue acceptance criteria added to Phase 1 plan
Plan references redis-and-job-queue-integration.md for details
grep -q 'job queue' phase-1-production-readiness.md returns 0

stories.title: Phase 2: Implement Redis cache with circuit breaker
acceptanceCriteria: backend/cache/redis_cache.py implements RedisCache extending AbstractCache
Redis connection pooling configured with redis.asyncio
CircuitBreaker class implements threshold=3, timeout=60 pattern
Circuit breaker tracks failures and opens after threshold
Circuit breaker falls back to SQLite when Redis unavailable
Redis cache get/set/delete operations use circuit breaker
CACHE_URL='redis://localhost:6379/0' config works
Test verifies cache falls back to SQLite on Redis failure

stories.title: Phase 2: Implement arq-based Redis queue
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/redis_queue.py implements RedisQueue via arq library
arq installed in requirements.txt
arq worker configured with max_jobs=1, job_timeout=300s
RedisQueue implements AbstractQueue interface (drop-in replacement)
Jobs can be enqueued to Redis and processed by arq workers
arq worker processes jobs from backend/queue/handlers.py
Test verifies Redis queue processes jobs correctly
Migration from SQLite to Redis tested

stories.title: Phase 2: Add queue latency monitoring
acceptanceCriteria: backend/monitoring/queue_metrics.py created
Queue latency p50, p95, p99 tracked and logged
Queue depth metric monitored (get_pending_count())
Job timeout rate tracked (timeouts / total jobs)
Error rate by job type tracked
Metrics logged or exposed for monitoring
Test verifies latency metrics collected accurately

stories.title: Phase 2: Create SQLite to Redis migration tool
acceptanceCriteria: backend/queue/migrate_to_redis.py created
Migration script drains SQLite queue before switching
Jobs migrate from SQLite to Redis without data loss
Hot migration supported (Redis worker takes over)
Rollback to SQLite tested and works
Migration can be reversed if needed
Test verifies migration completes successfully

stories.title: Update documentation with job queue architecture
acceptanceCriteria: README.md updated with job queue architecture section
README.md explains Phase 1 (SQLite) and Phase 2 (Redis) approach
README.md documents JOB_WORKER_ENABLED configuration
README.md documents crash recovery behavior
docs/architecture/adr-001-job-queue.md created with decision record
ADR documents SQLite-first decision and alternatives considered
ADR includes: Decision, Drivers, Alternatives, Why Chosen, Consequences, Follow-ups
Documentation verified for accuracy

stories.title: Polygon blockchain whale listener
acceptanceCriteria: backend/data/polygon_listener.py exists with PolygonListener class
PolygonListener subscribes to Polygon WebSocket RPC (config: POLYGON_WS_URL, default wss://polygon-rpc.com)
Filters for ConditionalTokens contract address (config: CONDITIONAL_TOKENS_ADDRESS)
Parses log events to extract market positionId and trade size
Threshold filter: only emits trades >= MIN_WHALE_TRADE_USD (default 1000)
Persists alerts via WhaleTransaction model in backend/models/database.py
Graceful disconnect/reconnect with exponential backoff (max 5 retries before erroring out)
WhaleTransaction model has fields: id, tx_hash, wallet, market_id, side, size_usd, block_number, observed_at
Async test backend/tests/test_polygon_listener.py mocks websocket and verifies threshold filter + persistence

stories.title: News feed aggregator
acceptanceCriteria: backend/data/feed_aggregator.py exists with FeedAggregator class
Configurable RSS feeds list (default: BBC, Reuters business, Federal Reserve, CoinDesk, Cointelegraph)
fetch_all() returns list of NewsItem dataclass {source, title, link, published_at, summary}
Uses feedparser library; deduplicates by link hash
Handles network errors gracefully (per-feed try/except, partial results allowed)
Test backend/tests/test_feed_aggregator.py mocks feedparser.parse and verifies dedup + error isolation

stories.title: Sentiment analyzer using existing AI provider
acceptanceCriteria: backend/ai/sentiment_analyzer.py exists with SentimentAnalyzer class
Uses existing backend/ai/base.py AI provider (Groq/Claude/custom) for sentiment scoring
analyze(text) returns SentimentResult {score: float -1..1, label: positive|negative|neutral, confidence: float}
analyze_batch(texts) processes in parallel via asyncio.gather
Daily budget enforcement: respects settings.AI_DAILY_BUDGET_USD
Test backend/tests/test_sentiment_analyzer.py mocks AI provider and verifies parsing + batch concurrency

stories.title: Dynamic whale discovery and scoring
acceptanceCriteria: backend/core/whale_scoring.py exists with calculate_whale_score(wallet_history) -> float [0..1]
Score formula: win_rate*0.35 + clamped_roi*0.30 + clamped_size*0.20 + clamped_freq*0.15
backend/core/whale_discovery.py exists with WhaleDiscovery.discover(min_trades=10) async method
Discovery seeds candidates from Polymarket leaderboard API or known WalletConfig rows
Persists results into existing WalletConfig.whale_score column (add column if missing)
Test backend/tests/test_whale_scoring.py covers edge cases: empty trades, all-loss, all-win, large size, formula correctness

stories.title: Risk manager
acceptanceCriteria: backend/core/risk_manager.py exists with RiskManager class
validate_trade(size, current_exposure, bankroll, confidence) -> RiskDecision {allowed: bool, reason: str, adjusted_size: float}
Enforces MAX_POSITION_FRACTION (default 0.05), MAX_TOTAL_EXPOSURE_FRACTION (default 0.50)
Slippage cap via SLIPPAGE_TOLERANCE (default 0.02)
Uses settings.DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT to short-circuit when daily losses exceed limit
Test backend/tests/test_risk_manager.py covers: oversize trade rejection, daily loss circuit, slippage adjustment, low confidence rejection

stories.title: Auto-trader with confidence gating
acceptanceCriteria: backend/core/auto_trader.py exists with AutoTrader class
execute_signal(signal, risk_manager) async method handling auto-approval and execution
AUTO_APPROVE_MIN_CONFIDENCE config (default 0.85) — trades above this auto-execute
Trades below threshold create a PendingApproval record (new table/column) for manual review
Uses existing polymarket_clob.clob_from_settings for live execution; paper mode skips network call
Records all decisions via record_decision() helper
Test backend/tests/test_auto_trader.py mocks risk_manager and clob; verifies auto-execute branch and pending-approval branch

stories.title: Arbitrage detector
acceptanceCriteria: backend/core/arbitrage_detector.py exists with ArbitrageDetector class
detect_yes_no_arb(market) finds markets where YES + NO price < 0.95 (correlated arbitrage)
detect_cross_market(market_a, market_b) finds same-event arb across platforms
calculate_profit_after_fees(opportunity, fee_pct=0.02) returns net profit
scan_all(markets) returns list of ArbOpportunity sorted by profit
Test backend/tests/test_arbitrage_detector.py covers: profitable arb, unprofitable after fees, no opportunity

stories.title: ML prediction engine scaffold
acceptanceCriteria: backend/ai/prediction_engine.py exists with PredictionEngine class (interface only — no training in this story)
predict(features: Dict[str, float]) -> Prediction {probability_yes: float, confidence: float, model_version: str}
Initial implementation uses a deterministic baseline (logistic of weighted features) so the interface is callable end-to-end
extract_features(market, signal_data) helper returns the canonical feature dict
backend/ai/training/__init__.py + data_collector.py + feature_engineering.py + model_trainer.py + model_evaluator.py exist as stubs with class signatures and docstrings
Test backend/tests/test_prediction_engine.py verifies predict() returns probability in [0,1] and confidence in [0,1] for sample features

stories.title: Live WebSocket hook + market view (frontend)
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts exists, returns {data, status, sendMessage}, reconnects on disconnect with exponential backoff
frontend/src/components/LiveMarketView.tsx exists, subscribes via useWebSocket, animates price changes (CSS transition)
useWebSocket connects to /ws/markets endpoint (existing _broadcast_event channel in backend/api/main.py)
Vitest test frontend/src/test/useWebSocket.test.ts mocks WebSocket and verifies reconnect logic + message dispatch
Vitest test frontend/src/test/LiveMarketView.test.tsx renders without crash and displays placeholder when no data

stories.title: Trading terminal page + opportunity scanner (frontend)
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/pages/TradingTerminal.tsx exists, lazy-loaded route /trading-terminal in App.tsx
TradingTerminal composes LiveMarketView + OpportunityScanner + WhaleActivityFeed
frontend/src/components/OpportunityScanner.tsx lists arbitrage opportunities sorted by profit
frontend/src/components/WhaleActivityFeed.tsx subscribes to /ws/whales channel and shows recent whale alerts
NavBar gets a Trading Terminal link
Vitest tests for TradingTerminal, OpportunityScanner, WhaleActivityFeed each render without crash

stories.title: Backend API endpoints for new features
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/whales/transactions returns recent WhaleTransaction rows (limit query param, default 50)
GET /api/arbitrage/opportunities returns ArbitrageDetector.scan_all output
GET /api/news/feed returns FeedAggregator.fetch_all() result
GET /api/predictions/{market_id} returns PredictionEngine.predict result for a market
POST /api/auto-trader/approve/{trade_id} approves a pending trade (require_admin auth)
All endpoints return JSON and have httpx tests in backend/tests/test_api_phase2.py

stories.title: PWA manifest + service worker
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/public/manifest.json exists with name, short_name, icons, theme_color, background_color, start_url
frontend/src/sw.ts exists implementing basic offline cache for static assets
frontend/index.html links the manifest with <link rel="manifest">
Service worker registers in frontend/src/main.tsx in production mode only (import.meta.env.PROD)
vite.config.ts builds successfully with PWA artifacts (npm run build exits 0)

stories.title: Wire new modules into orchestrator + scheduler
acceptanceCriteria: backend/core/orchestrator.py wires PolygonListener, FeedAggregator, AutoTrader, ArbitrageDetector with feature flags
Feature flags added to backend/config.py: WHALE_LISTENER_ENABLED, NEWS_FEED_ENABLED, AUTO_TRADER_ENABLED, ARBITRAGE_DETECTOR_ENABLED (all default False)
backend/core/scheduler.py adds optional jobs for news_feed_scan and arbitrage_scan when flags enabled
Modules only initialize when their flag is True (no import errors when flags off)
Test backend/tests/test_orchestrator_wiring.py imports orchestrator with all flags False and asserts no exceptions

stories.title: End-to-end smoke + verification
acceptanceCriteria: python -m pytest backend/tests/ -q runs to completion with 0 failures
npm --prefix frontend run test -- --run completes with 0 failures
npm --prefix frontend run build completes successfully
python -c 'from backend.api.main import app; print(len(app.routes))' runs without ImportError
All RQ-* and PE-* stories in this PRD are passes:true
